author: @bryanst.martin7134
text: You made that clear. And most are thinking, there's got to be a cheaper way! I argued with a Mate that we should go ally for a PI project. He argued it's expensive. I argued that the steel has metallurgy problems after welding and you cant easily coat the inside. Annual painting was expensive. Marine grade Ally was rather tolerant. He argued further, so I said how about the shipping cost? Guess who won? :-)

author: @bryanst.martin7134
text: The biggest problem with a Graphene balloon is leakage! Hyd and Helium are smaller than the holes in the Carbon structure...

author: @TomFarrell-p9z
text:
Great points!

As an aside, I understand the attraction of a graphene balloon.  For decades, while Europeans flew manned hydrogen balloons, the US had such a strategic reserve of helium we used helium balloons for manned flights.  (Around the world folks also fly hot air ballooons, but they're different.  Hot air balloons fly for an hour or so, gas balloons for up to several days.)  As the helium supply ran low, and prices increased, I helped friends of mine as they built and flew one of the first modern hydrogen balloons in the US, based on European designs.  To prevent sparks, the envelope has a carbon based conductive material surrounding the gas, and everything conductive in the balloon is grounded together.  The envelope is rather heavy, and I suspect graphene would be lighter.
